landmarks.name: 202 Connector Trail
landmarks.longDescription: This is a paved trail (with one short gravel segment) connecting the US-202 Parkway Trail to the Bradford Dam trail
landmarks.trailDistanceDescription: This is a paved trail (with one short gravel segment) connecting the US-202 Parkway Trail to the Bradford Dam trail
landmarks.description: This mostly-paved trail brings you from the 202 Parkway Trail to the Bradford Dam reservoir and the trail that goes through it

landmarks.name: Bluebird and cavity nesting bird program
landmarks.description: The Warrington Environmental Advisory Council is implementing a bluebird and cavity nesting bird program along this trail and throughout the Township.  Bird boxes have been installed at specific locations along this trail and throughout the Township to increase cavity bird populations.
landmarks.longDescription: The Warrington Environmental Advisory Council is implementing a bluebird and cavity nesting bird program along this trail and throughout the Township.  Bird boxes have been installed at specific locations along this trail and throughout the Township to increase cavity bird populations. Bluebirds and other cavity nesting birds such as tree swallows, screech owls, and kestral falcons provide many benefits to our community by eating insects and small rodents.

landmarks.name: Pollinator Habitat
landmarks.description: Pollinator Habitats are gardens that provides food, water and a safe living space for our native pollinators.
landmarks.longDescription: Pollinator Habitats are gardens that provides food, water and a safe living space for our native pollinators. This habitat has been created with different plants for a variety of pollinator species to feed on plant nectar, throughout the growing season.

landmarks.name: Pet Waste
landmarks.description: Pet waste contains harmful bacteria, viruses and parasites that can wash into streams, like the Neshaminy Creek.
landmarks.longDescription: Pet waste contains harmful bacteria, viruses and parasites that can wash into streams, like the Neshaminy Creek. These diseases can pose a health hazard to both people and pets. Picking up after your pet controls the spread of diseases to humans and wildlife and protects Warrington’s waterways. Thank you for bagging your pet’s waste, and putting it in the trash.

landmarks.name: Naturalized Basin
landmarks.description: Naturalized detention basin is a constructed dry pond that is designed to collect storm water runoff and uses native plant species to adsorb and filter pollutants and storm water.
landmarks.longDescription: Naturalized detention basin is a constructed dry pond that is designed to collect storm water runoff and uses native plant species to adsorb and filter pollutants and storm water. The benefits of natualizing storm water basins include reduced mowing, greater recharge to groundwater, reduced pollutants entering our streams and rivers and increase in wildlife habitat.

landmarks.name: Native Plant Management Area
landmarks.description: This corridor is being actively managed by the Warrington Environmental Advisory Council to promote native plants.
landmarks.longDescription: This corridor is being actively managed by the Warrington Environmental Advisory Council to promote native plants. Invasive plants are being controlled to prevent them from crowding out the native vegetation. Step off of the trail and explore. Many of the native plants have been identified, many have edible fruits and nuts. Please Enjoy.

landmarks.name: Rain Barrels
landmarks.description: Rain Barrels are barrels used to hold rain water that is collected from a roof. Rain barrels allows us to reuse rain water, allowing us to conserve water and save money.
landmarks.longDescription: Rain Barrels are barrels used to hold rain water that is collected from a roof. Rain barrels allows us to reuse rain water, allowing us to conserve water and save money.

landmarks.name: Bluebird and cavity nesting bird program
landmarks.description: The Warrington Environmental Advisory Council is implementing a bluebird and cavity nesting bird program along this trail and throughout the Township.  Bird boxes have been installed at specific locations along this trail and throughout the Township to increase cavity bird populations.
landmarks.longDescription: The Warrington Environmental Advisory Council is implementing a bluebird and cavity nesting bird program along this trail and throughout the Township.  Bird boxes have been installed at specific locations along this trail and throughout the Township to increase cavity bird populations. Bluebirds and other cavity nesting birds such as tree swallows, screech owls, and kestral falcons provide many benefits to our community by eating insects and small rodents.

landmarks.name: Native Plant vs Invasive Plants
landmarks.description: Native plants are plants that naturally existed in the area before colonization. nvasive plants are exotic plants that originated in other parts of the country or world.
landmarks.longDescription: Native plants are plants that naturally existed in the area before colonization. Native plants have been growing in the area for thousands of years and are adapted to local soil and climate, once established they need minimal maintence.    Invasive plants are exotic plants that originated in other parts of the country or world. Invasive plants have few predators and can quickly spread and are often harmful  to other plants and animals.

landmarks.name: Native Plant Management Area
landmarks.description: This corridor is being actively managed by the Warrington Environmental Advisory Council to promote native plants.
landmarks.longDescription: This corridor is being actively managed by the Warrington Environmental Advisory Council to promote native plants. Invasive plants are being controlled to prevent them from crowding out the native vegetation. Step off of the trail and explore. Many of the native plants have been identified, many have edible fruits and nuts. Please Enjoy.

landmarks.name: Pet Waste
landmarks.description: Pet waste contains harmful bacteria, viruses and parasites that can wash into streams, like the Neshaminy Creek.
landmarks.longDescription: Pet waste contains harmful bacteria, viruses and parasites that can wash into streams, like the Neshaminy Creek. These diseases can pose a health hazard to both people and pets. Picking up after your pet controls the spread of diseases to humans and wildlife and protects Warrington’s waterways. Thank you for bagging your pet’s waste, and putting it in the trash.

landmarks.name: Trees
landmarks.description: Trees improve biodiversity, sequester carbon, adsorb and filter pollutants from storm water.
landmarks.longDescription: Trees improve biodiversity, sequester carbon,   adsorb and filter pollutants from storm water. Trees provide food and habitat for many species of amimals. They help to reduce folloding and filter surface and ground water, removing many pollutants before the pollutants reach a stream. They capture and store carbon and filter air pollutnatnts and other bad odors out of the air.

landmarks.name: Wetlands
landmarks.description: Wetlands are areas where water covers the soil all or varying times of the year.
landmarks.longDescription: Wetlands are areas where water covers the soil all or varying times of the year, where the majority of plants that grow are water loving and where the subsoil is saturated most of the year.

landmarks.name: Fish
landmarks.description: The Neshaminy Creek contains Bluegill, Bass and Trout.
landmarks.longDescription: The Neshaminy Creek contains Bluegill, Bass and Trout. Storm water runoff from rain and snow melt can be polluted with sediments, pesticides, oil and other chemicals. These pollutants affect the fish in the water. Some contaminants can accumulate in their bodies. These contaminants can potentially be passed on to humans through the food chain.

landmarks.name: Meadows
landmarks.description: A meadow is an open field cover mostly by tall grasses.
landmarks.longDescription: A meadow is an open field cover mostly by tall grasses. Meadows provide a variety of benefits.  The benefits include providing habitat for many birds, insects and small animals.  Providing natural beauty from flowering plants. Meadows adsorb additional rain fall and runoff and reduce flooding.  They also sequester carbon and require less mowing.

landmarks.name: Waterfowl
landmarks.description: Waterfowl are ducks, geese or other large aquatic birds.
landmarks.longDescription: Waterfowl are ducks, geese or other large aquatic birds. Adult geese, goslings and eggs provide food for other animals. Please don't feed the wild birds, it can cause poor health, overcrowding and spread disease.

landmarks.description: This is the end of the trail
landmarks.longDescription: This is the end of the trail
